message:
Fix ANSI escape sequence handling for non-SGR CSI sequences

The visible_width and cut_line functions previously only recognized SGR
(Select Graphic Rendition) escape sequences ending in 'm' for colors.
This broke width calculation for other CSI sequences like:
- Clear screen (\x1b[2J)
- Cursor position (\x1b[H)
- Erase line (\x1b[K)

Changes:
- Add in_csi state to track CSI sequences separately from simple escapes
- CSI sequences now correctly terminate on any byte in 0x40-0x7E (@ through ~)
- Simple escape sequences (e.g., \x1b7 save cursor) consume single char after ESC
- Add comprehensive tests for non-SGR CSI and simple escape sequences

This ensures viewport scrolling and line cutting work correctly when the
content contains screen control sequences, not just color codes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
